content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

May 17, 1993


Charles P. Scully, II
Law Offices of Carroll & Scully, Inc.
300 Montgomery St., Ste. 735
San Francisco, CA 94104-1909

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-93-120

Dear Mr. Scully:

You have requested advice concerning the lobbying provisions
of the Political Reform Act (the "Act").1/ Your letter states
that informal assistance will be sufficient for your purposes.2/

QUESTION

Are you required to attend an orientation course on the
ethical issues and laws related to lobbying in connection with
your lobbyist registration for the 1993-94 Legislative Session?

CONCLUSION

You must attend an orientation course for the 1993-94
Legislative Session.

FACTS

You attended a lobbyist orientation course on December 13,
1990. On December 21, 1992, you executed a Lobbyist Certification
Statement (Form 604) as part of your lobbying firm's renewal of
registration.


1/ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

2/ Informal assistance does not provide the requestor with the
immunity provided by an opinion or formal written advice.
(Section 83114; Regulation 18329(c)(3).)


428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento, CA 95804-0807 • (916) 322-5660

Charles P. Scully, II
Our File No. I-93-120
Page 2


ANALYSIS

     In 1990, legislation was enacted to require lobbyists to
attend an orientation course on relevant ethical issues and laws
relating to lobbying. (Chapter 84, Stats. 1990.)  Upon filing a
certification Form 604, a lobbyist must indicate whether he or she
has taken the course within the previous 24 months.  (Section
86103(d)(1).)

     You have asked whether the phrase "within the previous 24
months" means within a 4 months of the month in which the lobbyist
completed the course or the day in which the course was completed.
We have interpreted Section 86103 to mean within 24 months of the
day in which the lobbyist completed a previous course.  In
addition, we believe the intent of Chapter 84 was that lobbyists
should attend the course during each legislative session.
Therefore, when you executed the Form 604 on December 21, 1992,
you had not completed the course within the previous 24 months and
you must attend a course as soon as possible.

     If you have additional questions or need further information,
please contact me at (916) 322-5662.

                                   Sincerely,

                                   [signature]

                                   Carla Wardlow
                                   Division Chief
                                   Technical Assistance Division

QUESTION: Are you required to attend an orientation course on the
ethical issues and laws related to lobbying in connection with
your lobbyist registration for the 1993-94 Legislative Session?

CONCLUSION: You must attend an orientation course for the
1993—94
Legislative Session.
